summary: v1.5 concrete-graph orientation-link extraction released
body: v1.5 reports +0.028 augmented_exact_k specificity gap and -0.147/-0.070 partial correlations on a parallel synthetic-deterministic graph corpus (58 graphs). Honesty caveat at release: rescue values come from v0.9 simulator runs but orientation overlap comes from a hash-keyed sampling of independent topology, so the +0.028 could be a sampling artifact. v1.1 audit machinery (decoupling 12/12; selector_stress=not_certification_channel; ledger_failure=ledger_control_not_resolved) runs end-to-end.

summary: v1.6 graph-coupled extraction released; reversed-sign rescue gaps reported
body: v1.6 imports the v0.9 simulator directly and computes orientation overlap on the same CausalDAG instance per trial, closing the v1.5 rescue/topology disconnection. Initial release reported reversed-sign specificity gaps -0.146 (at_least_k) and -0.229 (augmented_exact_k) for orientation_degradation; partial correlations -0.147/-0.070 from v1.5 reported as not reproducing. Initial framing claimed v1.5 specificity retracted.

summary: Witness-keying member_idx artifact identified and patched
body: User-prompted double-check identified that the v1.5/v1.6 graph_coupled_orientation_link_witness keyed sign on (depth(v)+depth(p)+member_idx)%2 plus an :m{member_idx%3} token tag. On regular topologies (chains etc.) with constant depth-parity, this forced sibling family members to produce disjoint witness sets and intra-family Jaccard ~ 0; per-cell output before fix had 18/24 cells with overlap=0 EXACTLY. Patched to (depth(v)+depth(p))%2 with no member tag; witness depends only on (DAG, cut). Same bug present in v1.5's ra_concrete_graph_orientation_extraction.py.

summary: v1.6 reframed as subset matched-graph diagnostic (not canonical)
body: User audit identified six accuracy/provenance issues. Addressed: (1) overlap statistic disambiguation - all-pairs primary with parent-anchored as auxiliary column; (2) partial-correlation narrative aligned with CSV (rescue residual variance nonzero for ledger/orientation cells, zero only for selector_stress controls); (3) "canonical" language replaced by "subset matched-graph diagnostic run, not canonical"; (4) specificity bins acknowledged as 4 rows per cell; (5) validation log regenerated non-empty (6 tests); (6) CLI exposes --severance-seeds / --modes / --severities / --threshold-fractions / --family-semantics for v1.7 scaling. Status fields flipped from canonical_run_* to matched_subset_run_*.

summary: v1.5 specificity initially marked superseded by v1.6 audit
body: Caveats stamped [SUPERSEDED 2026-05-05 by RA-SIM-GRAPH-COUPLED-ORIENT-002]. proof_status flipped to simulation_canonical_run_superseded_by_v1_6_audit. v1.6->v1.5 edge in claim_edges.csv reclassified from proof_dependency to corrigendum_supersession. Framing entry RA-SIM-V1-5-CORRIGENDUM-METHOD-001 added recording that v1.5 +0.028 specificity is retracted. NOTE: this supersession was later softened on 2026-05-06 (see EV-2026-05-06-002) when it was discovered that v1.6 still carries the same member_idx keying flaw.

summary: Commit 3b40be2 silently reverted the b742034 witness-keying fix
body: User audit (after deterministic-reproduction rerun) noted that the orientation-link witness still includes member_idx. Diff b742034..3b40be2 for analysis/ra_graph_coupled_orientation_extraction.py confirms the witness function lines 76-80 were reverted from `(depth(v) + depth(p)) % 2` (no member tag) back to `(depth(v) + depth(p) + member_idx) % 2` plus `:m{member_idx % 3}`. Cause: 3b40be2 overwrote the file from a re-installed packet copy that carried the pre-bug-fix witness; the audit-corrections commit message did not flag the regression.

summary: v1.5 and v1.6 both keying-tainted; neither cites as evidence; v1.7 ablation required
body: With the b742034 fix reverted by 3b40be2 (see EV-2026-05-06-001) and the same member_idx-keyed witness present in v1.5's ra_concrete_graph_orientation_extraction.py, both packets share the same potential keying artifact. Empirical numbers from either packet (v1.5 +0.028; v1.6 -0.146/-0.229) are not topology-causal conclusions. v1.6 does not cleanly retract v1.5; v1.5 is not cleanly confirmed; v1.6 matched-graph extraction methodology stands as a real advance, but its specificity/partial-correlation outputs are keying-tainted. proof_status on RA-SIM-CONCRETE-GRAPH-ORIENT-002, RA-SIM-GRAPH-COUPLED-ORIENT-001, and RA-SIM-GRAPH-COUPLED-ORIENT-002 flipped to simulation_keying_tainted_pending_v1_7_ablation. RA-SIM-V1-5-CORRIGENDUM-METHOD-001 rewritten to spec the v1.7 keying-ablation canonical sweep (six variants on the same matched-graph trials with case-1/2/3 success criteria).

summary: v1.7 canonical orientation-keying ablation sweep executed
body: User requested canonical run with --seed-start 17 --seed-stop 117 (100 seeds) --severance-seeds 101,103,107 --max-targets 12 --severities 0.25,0.5,0.75 --threshold-fractions 0.25,0.5,0.75 --family-semantics at_least_k,augmented_exact_k. Six orientation-link witness keyings tested: member_indexed_edge_pair, edge_pair_signed_no_member, edge_direction_only, incidence_role_signed, catalog_augmented_edge_pair (v1.3 native catalog tokens), shuffled_overlap_control. Run produced 194,400 base matched trials × 6 keyings = 1,166,400 keyed rows; 1,296 per-cell rows; 36 cells per (keying, mode, family_semantics). selector_guardrail_passed=true; any_positive_non_member_orientation_specificity=false. The trial_rows CSV is 763 MB and gitignored.

summary: v1.7 result: Case 1 - v1.5 specificity does not survive any keying; v1.6 reversal is binning artifact (shuffled control matches)
body: Specificity gaps for orientation_degradation: catalog_augmented_edge_pair -0.213/-0.184; edge_direction_only -0.211/-0.191; edge_pair_signed_no_member -0.211/-0.191; incidence_role_signed -0.211/-0.191; member_indexed_edge_pair -0.161/-0.102 (less reversed); shuffled_overlap_control -0.213/-0.188 (matches non-member graph keyings). Partial correlations after support+frontier OLS control for orientation_degradation: non-member graph keyings +0.047/-0.024; member_indexed +0.501/+0.271 on residual_std 0.007-0.016 (inflated by support tying); shuffled control +0.195/+0.138. Three findings: (1) v1.5 +0.028 specificity is RETRACTED at canonical scale - the positive signal does not appear under any tested matched-graph keying; (2) v1.6 reversed-sign verdict is NOT topology-causal anti-specificity - shuffled null control reproduces the same gap, indicating a binning/family-structure artifact; (3) member_idx is a real provenance issue (it ties orientation_overlap to support+frontier structure, near-zero residual_std) but is NOT the cause of the reversal - removing member_idx makes the gap MORE reversed, not less. Registry consequence: RA-SIM-CONCRETE-GRAPH-ORIENT-002.proof_status -> simulation_retracted_by_v1_7_canonical_ablation; RA-SIM-GRAPH-COUPLED-ORIENT-001.proof_status -> simulation_methodology_confirmed_canonical_via_v1_7; RA-SIM-GRAPH-COUPLED-ORIENT-002.proof_status -> simulation_canonical_artifact_corrigendum_resolved; RA-SIM-V1-5-CORRIGENDUM-METHOD-001 framing rewritten. Next step: v1.8 support_width/family-structure/binning confound audit (RA-ISSUE-V1-8-WIDTH-CONFOUND-AUDIT-001).

summary: v1.8 width-only confound audit run on canonical v1.7 outputs
body: Loaded ra_v1_7_keyed_trial_rows.csv (1.17M rows, 763 MB), tertile-binned orientation_overlap per (keying, mode, family_semantics), and computed low_minus_high gap within fixed support_width strata. Headline: graph keyings raw -0.198/-0.209 → width-matched -0.177/-0.180 (~10% reduction); shuffled control raw -0.193 → width-matched +0.171 for orientation_degradation/at_least_k (sign flips). Within-width comparison flagged graph keyings as differing from shuffled. Transient "Case B residual" reading prompted v1.8.1 joint stratification.

summary: v1.8.1 joint width × family-size matched audit confirms Case A; v1.7 reading restored
body: Extended ra_support_width_family_confound.py with run_joint_analysis, joint_matched_gaps, joint_aggregate, graph_vs_shuffled_joint, estimability_by_stratum, gap_decomposition. Strata: (mode, family_semantics, threshold_fraction, severity, support_width, family_size). Per (keying, stratum) tertile orientation_overlap and low_minus_high gap when both bins populated and orientation values are non-tied. Headlines: mean_abs_raw_gap_graph=0.129; mean_abs_width_matched_gap_graph=0.120 (7% explained); mean_abs_joint_matched_gap_graph=0.003 (101% explained, gap collapses). mean_abs_joint_matched_gap_shuffled=0.003. mean_abs_graph_minus_shuffled_joint=0.005. Per-cell: catalog_augmented_edge_pair / orientation_degradation joint-matched gap +0.010 (at_least_k) / +0.003 (augmented_exact_k); shuffled / orientation_degradation -0.001 / +0.007. graph_vs_shuffled_joint flagged catalog_augmented as graph_collapses_to_shuffled_after_joint_matching across all six cells. Pure-graph keyings (edge_pair_signed_no_member, edge_direction_only, incidence_role_signed) non-estimable in 0/36 strata - their orientation_overlap is structurally constant within fixed (mode, semantics, threshold, severity, support_width, family_size). v1.7 reading (gap is binning artifact) is now directly confirmed under joint stratification. v1.8 "Case B residual" was an artifact of width-only matching; v1.8.1 is controlling. RA-SIM-V1-5-CORRIGENDUM-METHOD-001 caveat updated to cite v1.8.1 instead of pending v1.8. RA-ISSUE-V1-8-WIDTH-CONFOUND-AUDIT-001 resolved by RA-SIM-CONFOUND-V18-1-001.

summary: Independent v1.8.1 packet's joint audit confirms binning artifact at higher rigor (Case C); cell-level tertile binning shows orientation overlap is nearly a deterministic function of (support_width x family_size)
body: User dropped in a parallel v1.8.1 packet (RA_CausalDAG_MotifCommitSimulator_v1_8_1_JointWidthFamilyConfound_May06_2026) with a different binning convention from my earlier v1.8.1 (which lived in the v1.8 packet's analysis module). The packet uses CELL-level tertile binning - tertile orientation_overlap per (keying, mode, family_semantics, severity, threshold_fraction) - rather than re-tertile-binning within each (mode, semantics, threshold, severity, support_width, family_size) stratum. Run: input_rows=1166400, keying_count=6, raw_gap_rows=324, joint_matched_gap_rows=324, estimable_joint_group_fraction_graph=0.0. The estimability table reveals the structural pattern directly: each (support_width, family_size) bucket produces a narrow range of orientation_overlap values that lands in 1-2 adjacent tertile bins, never spanning low and high. (1,1) -> low+medium; (2,3) -> high+medium; (3,4) -> high; (4,11/15) -> high. v1_8_1_posture: joint_width_family_matching_not_estimable_redesign_sampler_needed (Case C). This is a STRICTER reading than the previously-installed RA-SIM-CONFOUND-V18-1-001 (Case A under within-stratum re-binning). Both readings confirm 'no orientation-specific signal' but the packet's reading is the more methodologically conservative formulation: instead of 'gap collapses to ~0 within strata', the cell-level reading is 'the gap is non-estimable because orientation_overlap and (width, family_size) are nearly co-determined at this scale'. Registry consequence: added RA-SIM-CONFOUND-V18-1-PACKET-001 as a complementary canonical reading, cross-referencing RA-SIM-CONFOUND-V18-1-001/002 as alternative methodologies. Future audits should adopt cell-level tertiles (RA-SIM-CONFOUND-METHOD-001).

Added framing entry RA-SIM-V1-SERIES-SYNTHESIS-METHOD-001 as the canonical citation anchor. Extended RA-SIM-CONFOUND-METHOD-001 with rule (f) fixed-bin discipline: bins used to define the tested signal must remain fixed across confound controls; if joint strata lack both low and high bins under that fixed definition, the signal is non-estimable rather than rescued by local re-binning. Future orientation-overlap rescue discussions cite this synthesis, not the individual v1.5..v1.8.1 entries.

body: Ran the canonical Track B.2 extraction against the v0.9 NativeCertificateOverlap simulator: python scripts/run_trackB_active_graph_witness_extraction.py --v0-9-simulator-dir ../RA_CausalDAG_MotifCommitSimulator_v0_9_NativeCertificateOverlap_May05_2026/simulator --output-dir outputs --seed-start 17 --seed-stop 117 --steps 32 --max-targets 12 --threshold-fractions 1.0,0.75,0.5,0.25 --family-semantics at_least_k,augmented_exact_k. Canonical run results: 100 seeds (17..116); 24,130 member-level witness rows; 9,600 family-overlap rows; 9,600 member-index audit rows; 20 fixed-bin coverage cells; 18 (semantics, support_width, family_size) strata. graph_surface_member_index_free=true on every audit row. bad_control_detects_member_index_instability=true on every audit row. SUBSTANTIVE COVERAGE FINDING: active_orientation_overlap_bins='low;medium' only - no high-bin coverage; estimable_width_family_strata=0 of 18 - no stratum has both low and high bins on this configuration. Three augmented_exact_k strata reach two bins (low+medium): (3,4) (4,5) (4,7); all at_least_k strata are single-bin. Implication: the v0.9 native-certificate-overlap simulator at default configuration cannot, on its own, produce the matched-graph contrast required for orientation-specific rescue analysis across the full overlap range. The Track B.2 extractor is functionally correct; the missing piece is configuration sweep / wider DAG topologies. No orientation-specific rescue claim is made or restored. v1.9 native-witness six-requirement gate (RA-SIM-V1-SERIES-SYNTHESIS-METHOD-001) remains binding. summary: Track B.2 framing corrigendum: v0.9 is graph-state substrate only; v1.8.1 is the controlling audit standard
body: User-issued framing corrigendum to EV-2026-05-06-019. The Track B.2 canonical 100-seed run was reported as if v0.9 NativeCertificateOverlap default configuration were the controlling basis for the zero-estimable-strata finding. That conflates the substrate with the standard. The correct hierarchy: v0.9 simulator core generates graph/motif/support-family states; v1.7 supplies the matched-graph keying-ablation trial stream; v1.8.1 is the authoritative confound-audit discipline (joint stratification + cell-level fixed-bin discipline + shuffled controls + estimability gate; RA-SIM-V1-SERIES-SYNTHESIS-METHOD-001 + RA-SIM-CONFOUND-METHOD-001). The v1.8.1 acceptance question for Track B is not 'does default v0.9 produce low/medium/high orientation bins?' but 'can the extracted native graph/cut witness surface populate v1.8.1-valid low/high bins inside support_width x family_size strata?'. Required revision in registry: claim RA-SIM-GRAPH-CUT-WITNESS-ACTIVE-001 caveat now reframes the zero-estimable-strata finding as a smoke test against the v0.9 default config, not a verdict against orientation-specific rescue; correct phrasing is 'current v0.9 default-config sampler does not support valid v1.8.1-compliant Track B inference', not 'orientation rescue is absent'. Framing entry RA-SIM-GRAPH-CUT-WITNESS-ACTIVE-METHOD-001 caveat updated to make the substrate-vs-standard distinction explicit and to forbid citing Track B.2 outputs as orientation-rescue evidence under any reading. Track B.3 acceptance criteria (forward-looking; not implemented in this corrigendum): consume v1.7 canonical matched-trial structure; attach Track B graph/cut witnesses to those trials; apply v1.8.1 fixed-bin discipline; report estimability under support_width x family_size strata; do not assert orientation-rescue claims unless v1.8.1-valid strata exist. No artifact SHAs change; no Lean source change; no run rerun. Pure registry-text corrigendum. Memory recorded: feedback_v1_8_1_controls_orientation_rescue.md.

summary: Track B.3 v1.8.1-compliant witness coverage audit canonical run completed; 0 legitimate keyings reach coverage sufficiency
body: Ran the canonical Track B.3 coverage sweep: python scripts/run_trackB3_v181_witness_coverage.py --input-dirs ../RA_TrackB_ActiveSimulatorGraphWitnessExtraction_May06_2026/outputs,../RA_CausalDAG_MotifCommitSimulator_v1_7_OrientationKeyingAblation_May06_2026/outputs --output-dir outputs. Three pytest tests pre-pass. Inputs: 9,600 Track B.2 active-graph rows + 1,166,400 v1.7 keyed-trial rows = 1,176,000 normalized rows. Output: 464 joint strata; 111 estimable_low_high (23.9%); 108 (config x keying x mode x semantics x threshold) tuples reach all three orientation bins; posture string `v1_8_1_valid_comparison_domains_available`. Sampler-recommendation breakdown by keying class: catalog_augmented_edge_pair 18 rebalance_width_family_bins_or_sampler; edge_direction_only 18 rebalance; edge_pair_signed_no_member 18 rebalance; incidence_role_signed 18 rebalance; graph_cut_member_index_free (Track B.2) 8 increase_high_overlap_coverage; member_indexed_edge_pair (RETRACTED tainted keying from v1.5..v1.8.1) 18 coverage_sufficient_for_v1_8_1_audit; shuffled_overlap_control (designed null check) 18 coverage_sufficient_for_v1_8_1_audit. SUBSTANTIVE READING: ALL 36 coverage-sufficient cell-tuples are in the retracted member-indexed keying or the shuffled control. Zero legitimate native-witness keyings reach coverage sufficiency. The four legitimate matched-graph keyings each reach low+medium+high bins but no support_width x family_size joint stratum is estimable for v1.8.1 audit; they all need sampler rebalancing. The Track B.2 active-graph keying needs more high-bin coverage (consistent with EV-2026-05-06-019). Reading the headline posture string in isolation reproduces exactly the v1.5..v1.8.1 framing trap that the corrigendum EV-2026-05-06-020 was meant to prevent. PACKET BUG FIXED ON INSTALL: original analysis module had a recurring v1.x packet pattern - both _normalize_active_b2 and _normalize_v17 created the output DataFrame via `pd.DataFrame()` then assigned scalars (config_id, source, etc.) to that empty DataFrame BEFORE the first length-N Series assignment, leaving those columns as NaN after index extension. The first run reported source_count=0 which flagged the bug. Fix: pass `index=range(len(df))` to the DataFrame constructor in both normalizers (lines 47 and 75 of analysis/ra_v181_compliant_witness_coverage.py). Fix preserves all 3 packet-local pytests and changes only metadata labels - the headline 111/464 estimability and 36 coverage-sufficient finding are stable across the fix. Installed SHA 6b21b973ef083eec279458f575db3e8acbc6f193d79924e4e7f40efa883b7584 reflects the fix; packet-original SHA differs. New recurring packet bug pattern recorded for future packet authors: scalar-to-empty-DataFrame pandas assignment leaks NaN. No orientation-rescue claim is made or restored; v1.9 native-witness six-requirement gate (RA-SIM-V1-SERIES-SYNTHESIS-METHOD-001) and v1.8.1 confound discipline (RA-SIM-CONFOUND-METHOD-001) remain binding. Per EV-2026-05-06-020: v0.9 / Track B.2 supplied substrate; v1.8.1 supplied audit standard; this packet operates the standard against the substrate.

summary: Track B.3 framing tightening: explicit exclude-tainted-and-null rule; Track B.4 gated on successful B.3b
body: User-issued framing tightening to EV-2026-05-06-021. Three changes recorded: (1) Framing entry RA-SIM-GRAPH-CUT-WITNESS-COVERAGE-METHOD-001 caveat now states the EXPLICIT RULE: coverage sufficiency must be evaluated AFTER excluding tainted keyings (member_indexed_edge_pair) and null controls (shuffled_overlap_control); a configuration is legitimately coverage-sufficient only if at least one legitimate native-witness keying reaches sufficiency. (2) Claim RA-SIM-GRAPH-CUT-WITNESS-COVERAGE-001 next_tasks now record AUTHORIZED NEXT STEP = Track B.3b sampler/topology rebalance, NOT Track B.4 rescue analysis; Track B.4 is GATED on a successful Track B.3b being recorded in the registry first. (3) Track B.3b acceptance criteria recorded explicitly (six bullets: at least one legitimate keying coverage-sufficient; low+high coexist within support_width x family_size strata; rows-per-estimable-stratum exceeds a minimum threshold; member-indexed not counted; shuffled control not counted; no orientation-rescue claim made). Sweep parameters and expected output filenames recorded for forward reference. Memory recorded: feedback_coverage_sufficiency_excludes_tainted_null.md and project_track_b_work_order.md. No artifact SHAs change; no Lean source change; no run rerun. Pure registry-text tightening.

summary: Track B.3b sampler/topology rebalance canonical run completed; only incidence_role_signed reaches v1.8.1 coverage sufficiency narrowly
body: Ran the canonical Track B.3b sampler/topology rebalance: 108 configs (steps {32,48,64} x max_targets {12,18,24,36} x branch_probability {0.35,0.42,0.55} x wide_join_probability {0.55,0.72,0.85}) x 100 seeds (17..116) x 5 LEGITIMATE keyings (edge_pair_signed_no_member, edge_direction_only, incidence_role_signed, catalog_augmented_edge_pair, graph_cut_member_index_free) x 2 family_semantics x 4 threshold_fractions; 9,576,000 trial rows; min_rows_per_stratum=25. Tainted (member_indexed_edge_pair) and null (shuffled_overlap_control) keyings excluded by construction via the LEGITIMATE_KEYINGS / TAINTED_OR_NULL_KEYINGS module constants. Four packet-local pytests pre-pass. Result: 108 of 4,320 (config x keying x semantics x threshold) cells reach coverage_sufficient_legitimate; posture string `valid_trackB_orientation_coverage_candidate_found`; orientation_rescue_claim_made=false. KEYING-RESOLVED BREAKDOWN: incidence_role_signed 108/864 sufficient (216 increase_high_overlap, 540 rebalance_width_family_bins); the other four legitimate keyings 864/864 increase_high_overlap_coverage. WORKING ENVELOPE: 108 sufficient cells span every (steps, max_targets, branch_probability, wide_join_probability) combination but are concentrated in family_semantics=augmented_exact_k AND threshold_fraction=0.25 only. Joint-strata depth shallow: max_sufficient_joint_strata_count=2 (88 cells with 2; 20 cells with 1). High-bin presence rate within sufficient cells: median 0.172 / range 0.063-0.311. STRUCTURAL HYPOTHESIS for why only incidence_role_signed reaches sufficiency: it is the only legitimate keying that omits the through-link `through:p->v->c:turn:parity` token family in tokens_for_keying, depending only on per-edge sign-and-depth-delta tokens. The four other keyings include the through-link family which dominates token volume and reduces overlap variance. This may be a sampler-design artifact rather than a discovery about orientation rescue. SIX-BULLET ACCEPTANCE (per EV-2026-05-06-022): (1) at least one legitimate keying coverage-sufficient -> incidence_role_signed YES; (2) low+high coexist within support_width x family_size -> YES (estimable_low_high=true); (3) rows-per-estimable-stratum >=25 -> YES (sufficient_joint_strata_count >0); (4) member-indexed not counted -> YES (excluded by LEGITIMATE_KEYINGS); (5) shuffled-control not counted -> YES (excluded by LEGITIMATE_KEYINGS); (6) no orientation-rescue claim -> YES. AUTHORIZED B.4 SCOPE locked: any future Track B.4 matched-graph orientation-rescue analysis must (i) restrict comparison domain to incidence_role_signed x augmented_exact_k x threshold_fraction=0.25; (ii) consume the 108 specific config_ids from ra_trackB3b_recommended_configurations.csv; (iii) pre-register shallow joint-strata depth as a power constraint; (iv) report nulls as readily as positives. Broad-spectrum orientation-rescue claims across keyings or thresholds are NOT licensed. If B.4 power is judged inadequate even after the optional B.3c deepening sweep within the working envelope, defer to B.4a generator redesign rather than attempt rescue analysis on inadequate power. PACKET INTEGRITY: SHA bbc98113 for the analysis module; no installation-time source fixes were required. Demo outputs from the original packet (16 small configs, 0 sufficient legitimate cells, max_high_bin_presence 0.45) were overwritten by this canonical run; original demo state is described in the report file. v1.9 native-witness six-requirement gate (RA-SIM-V1-SERIES-SYNTHESIS-METHOD-001) and v1.8.1 confound discipline (RA-SIM-CONFOUND-METHOD-001) remain binding.

summary: Track B.4 locked-envelope analysis canonical run; not estimable under joint strata; graph byte-identical to shuffled control at fixed-bin level
body: Ran the canonical Track B.4 locked-envelope rescue diagnostic: python scripts/run_trackB4_locked_envelope_rescue.py --input-dirs ../RA_CausalDAG_MotifCommitSimulator_v1_7_OrientationKeyingAblation_May06_2026/outputs,../RA_TrackB3b_OrientationWitnessSamplerRebalance_May06_2026/outputs --output-dir outputs --min-rows-per-bin 25. Inputs: 1,166,400 v1.7 keyed-trial rows + 9,576,000 B.3b sampler-sweep rows = 10,742,400 input rows. After locked-envelope filter (incidence_role_signed or shuffled_overlap_control + augmented_exact_k + threshold=0.25): 304,200 envelope rows (271,800 graph + 32,400 control); rescue column used certification_rescue_event; overlap column used orientation_overlap (with v1_7_orientation_overlap_all_pairs coalesced as fallback per the install-time normalizer fix). estimable_joint_strata_graph=0; estimable_joint_strata_control=0; selector_guardrail_passed=True; orientation_rescue_claim_made=False; posture=locked_envelope_not_estimable_under_joint_strata. AUXILIARY FINDING for paper writers (NOT a rescue claim): in the locked envelope, incidence_role_signed and shuffled_overlap_control produce BYTE-IDENTICAL fixed-bin gap structures across all (mode, severity) tuples - same low/medium/high row counts (1374/~600/1581) and same low_rescue_rate values to 6 decimals. ledger_failure 0.50: 0.341339 in both; orientation_degradation 0.75: 0.368996 in both; selector_stress: 0.0 in both. The graph keying does NOT move the rescue analysis relative to the shuffled null control in this envelope. PACKET BUGS FIXED ON INSTALL (two new recurring v1.x packet bug patterns): (1) Multi-source CSV column-collision: the original normalize_rows used `_find_col` to pick the FIRST matching candidate (`keying`/`orientation_overlap`), and the v1.7 rows that ship those values under different names (`v1_7_keying`/`v1_7_orientation_overlap_all_pairs`) were left with NaN in the chosen column after pd.concat - silently excluding the entire 32,400 graph + 32,400 control rescue-bearing v1.7 slice from the locked envelope. The fix coalesces all candidate columns per-row using `.fillna(out[c])` so each row picks up the value from whichever candidate is non-null. Recorded as memory feedback_packet_normalizer_keying_collision.md. (2) Empty-DataFrame KeyError: when no joint stratum is estimable, weighted_joint_gap returns an empty DataFrame; `weighted[weighted[\keying\"] == LOCKED_KEYING]` then raises KeyError because the empty DataFrame has no columns. Fix: explicit empty-or-no-columns guard before keying-based filters. Both fixes preserve all four packet-local pytests. AUTHORIZED NEXT STEPS: per project_track_b_work_order.md - if Track B.4 power inadequate
evidence_artifact_ids: route via optional Track B.3c (deepening sweep within the working envelope to add knobs not exercised in B.3b: max_parents
column12: family-construction breadth) or Track B.4a (orientation-diverse graph/family generator redesign). Do NOT attempt to make a rescue claim from the existing locked-envelope data. The fixed-bin gap of 0.18-0.37 in incidence_role_signed at higher severities is NOT a graph-keying signal - it matches the shuffled control byte-for-byte and reflects width/family/severity-level confounds. v1.9 native-witness six-requirement gate (RA-SIM-V1-SERIES-SYNTHESIS-METHOD-001) and v1.8.1 confound discipline (RA-SIM-CONFOUND-METHOD-001) remain binding."

summary: Track B.4 byte-identical-graph-vs-control finding RETRACTED; was an OVERLAP_CANDIDATES column-priority bug that binned on the keying-agnostic native overlap column
body: User-issued audit caught a suspicious finding from EV-2026-05-06-024: the claim that incidence_role_signed and shuffled_overlap_control produced byte-identical fixed-bin gap structures across all 9 (mode severity) tuples. Investigation traced the artifact to a third packet bug (beyond the two fixed in EV-2026-05-06-024). ROOT CAUSE: the v1.7 keyed-trial-rows CSV ships BOTH a keying-AGNOSTIC native simulator overlap column called `orientation_overlap` (a per-trial property invariant across the 6 keying replicas) AND a keying-SPECIFIC column `v1_7_orientation_overlap_all_pairs` (different value per keying). The original OVERLAP_CANDIDATES = ['orientation_overlap', 'v1_7_orientation_overlap_all_pairs', ...] in ra_trackB4_locked_envelope_rescue.py put the native column first. `_find_col` returned `orientation_overlap` as primary, the coalescing fallback only filled NaN cells (rows missing the primary), so for v1.7 rows BOTH columns are populated and the script used the native one. Result: the binning column did not depend on keying, so graph and control rows were partitioned identically into low/medium/high bins regardless of which keying they were tagged with - producing the byte-identical pattern at 6-decimal precision. EVIDENCE: (1) probing the v1.7 file directly shows incidence_role_signed × ledger_failure × severity=0.5 has overlap distribution {0.0: 1581, 0.1: 660, 0.166667: 714, 0.333333: 645} - quartile binning (1/3, 2/3) gives 1581 low / 660 medium / 1359 high; (2) the script's recorded fixed_bin_gaps.csv showed 1374 low / 645 medium / 1581 high with overlap_value=1.0 for 1581 rows - the 1.0 came from the simulator's native `orientation_overlap` column, not the v1.7 keying-specific column; (3) per-trial merge graph<->control showed orientation overlap differs across keyings (44% identical, correlation 0.35), confirming v1.7 IS keying-aware and the native column was the wrong one. FIX APPLIED: reorder OVERLAP_CANDIDATES to put keying-specific columns first - ['v1_7_orientation_overlap_all_pairs', 'v1_6_graph_coupled_orientation_overlap', 'graph_cut_orientation_overlap', 'orientation_overlap']. Re-ran canonical pipeline; all 4 packet-local pytests still pass; analysis SHA changed from a3bff4e8 to 8510bf01. CORRECTED RESULT: estimable_joint_strata_graph=0 (graph has only 4 unique overlap values per cell - too coarse for tertile binning at min_rows_per_bin=25); estimable_joint_strata_control=27/36 (control has 254 unique values per cell); control_mean_joint_gap=0.011 (near-zero noise consistent with null); graph_mean_joint_gap=None (graph not estimable); posture=`locked_envelope_not_estimable_under_joint_strata` (same string but for the right reason - graph overlap-value SPARSITY, not graph keying matching control). RETRACTED text from EV-2026-05-06-024: the claim that 'graph and shuffled control produce byte-identical fixed-bin gap structures across all (mode severity) tuples' is FALSE - it was the column-priority bug. Updated artifact SHAs for fixed_bin_gaps, joint_stratum_gaps, locked_envelope_trials, summary CSV/MD, graph_vs_shuffled_controls (now non-empty), width_family_matched_gap (now non-empty), power_coverage, canonical_run log. New recurring v1.x packet bug pattern recorded: column candidate priority. When a source ships both a keying-agnostic native column and a keying-specific column, ALWAYS bin on the keying-specific one. Memory recorded: feedback_packet_overlap_column_priority.md. orientation_rescue_claim_made remains False; v1.9 native-witness gate and v1.8.1 confound discipline remain binding; authorized next steps remain Track B.3c (envelope deepening targeting graph overlap-value sparsity) or Track B.4a (generator redesign).
